ip_outcome_note: ip_verified already true in frontmatter; no IP work required.

trivia_bullets.text: Chandra's Magmutt is a first-printing original to Core Set 2021 (reprint: false per Scryfall). It carries the type line 'Creature — Elemental Dog,' benefiting directly from M21's landmark Hound-to-Dog creature type rename. Adam Prosak, who put M21 together, proposed the rename and noted it faced no significant internal resistance — Mark Rosewater had advocated for the change for over 20 years. [Wizards of the Coast: 'But Wait, There's Core' 2020-06-15; Dotesports: 'MTG celebrates Dog name change with M21']

Core Set 2021 was designed around Chandra Nalaar as its featured planeswalker, with no storyline or Story Spotlights — the set functions as a mechanical showcase rather than a narrative chapter. Chandra's Magmutt and Chandra's Pyreling (M21 138) are both named-companion creatures in this Chandra-focused set. [Flipside Gaming: 'Core Set 2021 Design Review: The Planeswalkers'; Scryfall: story_spotlight=false]

"Is it purebred? No, but it's pure fire." — Chandra Nalaar. [Scryfall] The quote is voiced in Chandra's own register — self-deprecating humor about her elemental companion's mixed-elemental nature. The Magmutt's flavor text is one of only a handful in M21 attributed to Chandra herself.

EDHREC rank 14,007 [Scryfall: edhrec_rank]. A 2/2 for {1}{R} with a repeatable {T}: deal 1 damage to a player or planeswalker. Legal in Pauper and Commander. The tapping pinger ability creates minor inevitability in slower games; the card sees more play in Chandra-themed Commander builds than in competitive formats.
